free_text: Study of deuteron induced reactions on natural iron
and copper and their use for monitoring beam
parameters and for thin layer activation technique
Revised Cu(d,x)65Zn and Fe(d,x)56Co data tabulated
Same Fe(d,n)57Co data in fig.
,cyclotron lab in Debrecen.
, cyclotron lab. in Brussels
25 micron Fe and 10 micron Cu foils from Goodfellow
5 mm collimated beam on target.
The bombarding beam intensity was monitored continously
during the irradiation and was kept constant whithin 5%
Charge was collected in Faraday-cup and was monitored
with 27-AL(D,X)24-NA monitor reaction. The difference
was 10%. In the calculation of cross section data the
charge integrated by Faraday-cup was used. Nuclear
data were taken from E.Browne and R.B.Firestone, Table
of radioactive isotopes, ed.V.S.Shirley, Wiley 1986.
The initial energy was determined by time-of-
flight method at Brussels cyclotron with an accuracy
of 0.3 MeV, and by analysing magnet in Debrecen with
0.2 MeV accuracy. The effective energy was calculated
for each target foil using the energy range formula of
H.H.Andersen, J.F.Ziegler, Hydrogen stopping powers and
ranges in all elements, New York. Pergamon Press 1977.
The error of the energy scale was estimated taking
into account the uncertainty of the primary beam
energy, the straggling effect and the error of the
target thickness.
Data error is the square root of the sum
of the square of the individual errors. The average
error varied between 10% and 15%.
BIB updated. Ref. corrected.
KB New reference and new subentries
D4044005 and D4044006 were added which contain revised
data for 65Zn and 56Co.
Subentries D4044002 and D4044003 were superseded.
On. Major revisions in 002-005.
